findings_summary: Dashboard/API specify-route guard smoke passed: vFinal parent cards and worker subtasks cannot be rewritten by the generic specifier route, while ordinary disposable task specify still works through a fake auxiliary specifier.
tool_or_profile: Hermes v0.16.0 disposable installed runtime with vFinal adapter patches including candidate 0014
runtime_scope: Disposable installed Hermes checkout and isolated HERMES_HOME/HOME; direct Python calls into dashboard plugin route functions; no production dashboard server.

parent.detail: Overkill Factory cards and worker subtasks cannot be specified from dashboard/API; task contract changes must go through an explicit operator-safe specification workflow.

worker.detail: Overkill Factory cards and worker subtasks cannot be specified from dashboard/API; task contract changes must go through an explicit operator-safe specification workflow.

important_limitations: This proves direct dashboard plugin route functions in a disposable installed Hermes runtime after candidate patch 0014.
The positive normal-task path uses a fake auxiliary specifier to avoid real model calls.
It does not start an authenticated HTTP dashboard server.
It does not prove real auxiliary model quality.
It does not authorize updating a real Hermes runtime.
next_action: Add patch 0014 to the update sequence and continue proving remaining dashboard/API route families.
